title: Weather Update: Alarm in Maharashtra! IMD's Severe Weather Warning for the Next 48 Hours - Lokshahi English News
full_text:
Weather Update: Alarm in Maharashtra! IMD's Severe Weather Warning for the Next 48 Hours
The weather in the state has been extremely unstable for the past few days, showcasing a dual picture of a heatwave and the threat of rain. According to the warnings issued by the Indian Meteorological Department, a heatwave is expected along with hot and humid weather in the Konkan region for the next two days. Cyclonic winds are currently active over Maharashtra, which will be felt on the Konkan coast, with temperatures in Mumbai and Konkan reaching as high as 41 degrees Celsius. This situation is likely to persist for the next two days. ( Maharashtra Weather)
Considering the risk of rising temperatures, the Animal Husbandry Department has instructed all systems to remain alert. Farmers are advised to keep animals in the shade to protect them from the harsh sun, provide sufficient and clean drinking water, and avoid transporting animals during peak afternoon hours, as stated by the authorities. Citizens are also advised to provide water for animals and birds on the roads and to utilize local veterinary hospitals or the emergency helpline number 1962 for any urgent assistance, as clarified by the department.
In Vidarbha and central Maharashtra, the impact of the heat has intensified. In Jalgaon, temperatures have reached 43 degrees Celsius, leaving residents feeling overwhelmed by the rising heat.
In Malegaon, Nashik district, a temperature of 42.8 degrees Celsius has been recorded, with the streets and markets being deserted from 12 to 4 PM.
In Parbhani, temperatures have also reached 43 degrees Celsius, but daily life there appears to be relatively normal. Meanwhile, in Amravati, temperatures have peaked at 44 degrees Celsius. The meteorological department has warned that the heatwave will persist in many parts of the state for the next few days, and the district administration has urged citizens to take care of their health.
